git.baselineSubject: feat(gen34): Hydra View — live sub-tab grid + human labels + fanOut fires (#67)

cosmeticCatchesInOverlayTrio.note: Consolidated into single callOverlay helper in playwright.ts. Remaining 21 are in cursor-overlay.ts (11 needed for in-page DOM script) + fan-out.ts (10 — page.screenshot streamer + page.close + await call paths are each legitimate failure surfaces).

circularDeps.note: madge flags runner.ts<->fan-out.ts and runner.ts<->parallel-runner.ts as cycles, but the back-edges are `await import(...)` dynamic references (runtime-resolved, not module-load cycles). Refactored to structural ctor types + inline `await import` so there is no static type cycle either. Remaining madge warning is a false positive.

applied.change: Dynamic-import BrowserAgent in fan-out.ts and parallel-runner.ts
applied.rationale: Break the runner.ts <-> sub-agent-spawn cycle statically. runner.ts already dynamic-imports these modules; making the back-edge dynamic aligns both sides.

8 overlay methods each carried 10 lines of identical defensive boilerplate (install-await + try/catch + method-existence guard + page.evaluate catch). Consolidated to one helper + 8 one-liners. Single source of truth for the 'never break a run' defensive pattern.

applied.change: Extract demo-mode override to its own module
applied.rationale: BAD_FORCE_FANOUT_* env-var override was inline in runner.ts main loop. Demo scaffolding in production-path code. Moved to src/runner/demo-overrides.ts with a pure function API; runner.ts calls one function that returns unchanged in production. +8 test cases pin the behavior.

applied.change: Drop quote marks from overlay labels + handle leading ARIA prefixes
applied.rationale: Demo feedback: labels read like code (`Typing "JOHN" into Enter name as search crit...`) not prose. Dropped curly quotes; added leading-prefix strip (Enter/Type/Please/Fill in); drop label entirely when target name is a full sentence. +2 regression tests pin the OFAC 'Enter name as search criteria' case.

deferred.reason: all under bench/ and .evolve/scripts/ — invoked dynamically by --config or CLI flags knip can't see. Low value, high risk of deleting something real.
Public-ish interfaces (LibOptions, *Result types) exported for future/external callers. Deleting them triggers API churn, not signal.
Most are `any` inside page.evaluate callbacks — required because page context doesn't have our types. Documented in callOverlay with a single eslint-disable.
Loaded dynamically during design-audit runs. Verified before deleting.
